calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Confirming Trust Asset
Off Calendar Sep-19-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Peterson for Anne Sousanis (Trustee).

The Court thanks Mr. Peterson for providing further briefing, denied the request for a statement of decision, spoke of its thoughts regarding venue, indicated that venue is proper in San Francisco and spoke of its thoughts with respect to Ms. Roberts' disclaimer and the issue of the joint tenancy. The Court granted the Petition for Order Confirming Trust Asset (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Order Confirming Trust Asset
Off Calendar Aug-21-2019 Continued To Sep-18-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Confirming Trust Asset
Off Calendar Jul-24-2019 Continued To Aug-21-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Peterson for Anne Sousanis.

The Court noted that there is an issue of proper venue, that the notice of trustee regarding principal place of administration was filed and served on the Beneficiaries yesterday after the issue was raised by the Probate Examiner and that it should have been done immediately. Mr. Peterson stated that he thought it had been done by the other Attorney, that he believes the Trustee is allowed to choose her place of administration where she receives her mail, that she chooses to use his office and that the property is in San Francisco. The Court stated that it is not sure it found the memorandum of points and authority very helpful, that it does not answer the question of whether transferring the property into the trust severs the joint tenancy and questions if Mr. Peterson's answer to that is no. Mr. Peterson stated that the joint tenancy was not severed, made reference to Civil Code 683.2(c), stated that there was no document in writing and nothing was recorded, that by the Decedent creating the trust he did not sever joint tenancy. The Court stated that the laws of joint tenancy control distribution, that the tentative ruling is that Susan disclaimed her interest after Louis died, that at the time when she signed the disclaimer the property was owned by her and not the trust, and provided Counsel the opportunity to provide further briefing. The Court also stated that it does not see how someone can disclaim property that she solely owns. Mr. Peterson stated that the deed was done to Susan Roberts because she loaned money to purchase the property and was put on the property, and that nor the trust nor the deed were recorded. The Court stated that the Settlor may have put the property in the trust, but it remained in the character of joint tenancy. Mr. Peterson stated that the Decedent said he wanted the property in the trust, but did not record it. The Petition for Order Confirming Trust Asset is continued to August 21, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Peterson to provide more information regarding venue and further briefing on the issue of joint tenancy.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
